tests.source_program:
define i32 @test_computed_allones(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %notx = xor i32 %x, -1
  %allones = xor i32 %notx, %x
  %and1 = and i32 %allones, %y
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %and1, %y
  %result = select i1 %cmp, i32 1, i32 0
  ret i32 %result
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define i32 @test_computed_allones(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %notx = xor i32 %x, -1
  %allones = xor i32 %notx, %x
  %and1 = and i32 %allones, %y
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %and1, %y
  %result = select i1 %cmp, i32 1, i32 0
  ret i32 %result
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: The all-ones value is computed through xor operations rather than being a literal -1 constant. The summary mentions -1 but doesn't cover computed all-ones patterns that m_AllOnes() would still match.

define i32 @test_computed_allones(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %notx = xor i32 %x, -1
  %allones = xor i32 %notx, %x
  %and1 = and i32 %allones, %y
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %and1, %y
  %result = select i1 %cmp, i32 1, i32 0
  ret i32 %result
}

tests.source_program:
define i32 @test_ne_predicate_variant(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %and1 = and i32 %x, %y
  %cmp = icmp ne i32 %and1, %y
  %notcmp = xor i1 %cmp, true
  %result = select i1 %notcmp, i32 1, i32 0
  ret i32 %result
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define i32 @test_ne_predicate_variant(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %and1 = and i32 %x, %y
  %cmp = icmp ne i32 %and1, %y
  %notcmp = xor i1 %cmp, true
  %result = select i1 %notcmp, i32 1, i32 0
  ret i32 %result
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Uses icmp ne with explicit negation instead of icmp eq. The summary only discusses icmp eq patterns, but the all-ones guard would apply after canonicalization to eq form.

alive2.src:
define i32 @test_ne_predicate_variant(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %and1 = and i32 %x, %y
  %cmp = icmp ne i32 %and1, %y
  %notcmp = xor i1 %cmp, true
  %result = select i1 %notcmp, i32 1, i32 0
  ret i32 %result
}
alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define i32 @test_ne_predicate_variant(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
  %and1 = and i32 %x, %y
  %cmp.not = icmp eq i32 %and1, %y
  %result = zext i1 %cmp.not to i32
  ret i32 %result
}

tests.source_program:
define i64 @test_i64_width_variant(i64 %a, i64 %b, i64 %c) {
  %and1 = and i64 %a, %b
  %cmp1 = icmp eq i64 %and1, %b
  %and2 = and i64 %a, %c
  %cmp2 = icmp eq i64 %and2, %c
  %result = select i1 %cmp1, i64 0, i64 1
  ret i64 %result
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define i64 @test_i64_width_variant(i64 %a, i64 %b, i64 %c) {
  %and1 = and i64 %a, %b
  %cmp1 = icmp eq i64 %and1, %b
  %and2 = and i64 %a, %c
  %cmp2 = icmp eq i64 %and2, %c
  %result = select i1 %cmp1, i64 0, i64 1
  ret i64 %result
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary only mentions i32 examples; this test uses i64 integer width which is not explicitly covered in the summary text
